title: Switch release-tools tekton task to cosign-signing-key-images
description:
ONE-OFF (not part of reusable molecule) — repoint the existing release-tools signing task at the new image-only secret.

Steps:
- Locate the task that signs the release-tools image (candidates: .tekton/release-tools/tasks/cosign-attach-sbom.yaml or equivalent under .tekton/)
- Change every secret reference from cosign-signing-key to cosign-signing-key-images:
  - workspaces / volumes / secretName fields
  - env var sources (cosign.password)
  - any task params hard-coding the secret name
- Re-run the pipeline; verify image signature is produced by the new key (`cosign verify --key cosign-image.pub <image>`)

Context: Parent op1st-emea-b4mad-yn5. This is the actual cut-over point at which release-tools loses access to the artifact key.

Note: flagged as ONE-OFF — do NOT pull this step into the future beads molecule; the equivalent step in a reused workflow will target a different consumer.
acceptance_criteria:
- All references in release-tools tekton task updated to cosign-signing-key-images
- Pipeline run succeeds and produces a valid signature
- `cosign verify` with cosign-image.pub passes on the produced image
- grep over .tekton/release-tools shows no remaining reference to the old cosign-signing-key for image signing

title: Add emberstack reflector rule for cosign-signing-key-images
description:
Replicate the new image-signing Secret from op1st-gitops into op1st-pipelines (and any other consumer ns), using the same reflector pattern already applied to cosign-signing-key.

Steps:
- On the inner Secret template (spec.template.metadata.annotations of the SealedSecret), set:
  - reflector.v1.k8s.emberstack.com/reflection-allowed: "true"
  - reflector.v1.k8s.emberstack.com/reflection-allowed-namespaces: <regex matching cosign-signing-key, currently includes op1st-pipelines>
  - reflector.v1.k8s.emberstack.com/reflection-auto-enabled: "true"
  - reflector.v1.k8s.emberstack.com/reflection-auto-namespaces: <same regex>
- Copy verbatim from cosign-signing-key.enc.yaml so behaviour is identical
- Note: seal.sh re-merges plaintext annotations onto SealedSecret template (kubeseal strips them otherwise)
- Apply via ArgoCD; verify reflector controller logs and Secret presence in op1st-pipelines

Context: Parent op1st-emea-b4mad-yn5.
acceptance_criteria:
- Reflector annotations present on inner Secret template
- Replicated Secret visible in op1st-pipelines (and any other target ns)
- emberstack-reflector pod logs show successful copy (no errors)
- Annotation regex matches the same target set as cosign-signing-key

title: Create cosign-signing-key-images SealedSecret in op1st-pipelines
description:
Wrap the new image-signing keypair into a SOPS-encrypted plaintext Secret + kubeseal'd SealedSecret manifest in manifests/applications/op1st-pipelines-tokens/.

Pattern (per op1st-pipelines-tokens/README.md):
- Canonical namespace: op1st-gitops
- File pair: cosign-signing-key-images.enc.yaml (SOPS) + cosign-signing-key-images.yaml (SealedSecret)
- Sealing tool: ./seal.sh in that directory (iterates *.enc.yaml, kubeseal, re-merges annotations)

Steps:
- Author cosign-signing-key-images.enc.yaml with stringData: cosign.key, cosign.password (empty), cosign.pub
- Apply reflector annotations on spec.template.metadata (see bead k8s)
- sops -e in place, or copy structure from cosign-signing-key.enc.yaml
- Run ./seal.sh to produce SealedSecret yaml
- Add to kustomization.yaml
- Update README.md table to list cosign-signing-key-images
- ArgoCD sync; verify Secret materializes in op1st-gitops and reflects to op1st-pipelines

Context: Parent op1st-emea-b4mad-yn5. Mirrors the existing cosign-signing-key pattern exactly.
acceptance_criteria:
- cosign-signing-key-images.enc.yaml + .yaml committed under manifests/applications/op1st-pipelines-tokens/
- kustomization.yaml updated
- README.md table updated
- Decrypts cleanly into Secret cosign-signing-key-images in op1st-gitops
- ArgoCD reports application healthy/synced
- No plaintext key material in the repo

title: Generate dedicated cosign keypair for release-tools image signing
description:
Generate a new password-less cosign keypair to be used exclusively for signing the release-tools container image, isolating it from artifact signing material.

Pattern (per manifests/applications/op1st-pipelines-tokens/README.md):
- Canonical SealedSecret namespace is op1st-gitops; reflector mirrors into op1st-pipelines.
- Keys are password-less: COSIGN_PASSWORD="".
- Public key also published as plain file alongside .enc.yaml for verifier consumption.

Steps:
- cd manifests/applications/op1st-pipelines-tokens
- COSIGN_PASSWORD="" cosign generate-key-pair
- Rename outputs: cosign.key -> cosign-image.key, cosign.pub -> cosign-signing-key-images.pub
- Stage cosign-image.key locally for subsequent SealedSecret authoring; do NOT commit raw key
- Commit only the .pub (cosign-signing-key-images.pub) plus subsequent .enc.yaml / .yaml from later beads

Context: Parent op1st-emea-b4mad-yn5. Existing cosign-signing-key was migrated to password-less in commit 9cc648a; new key follows same convention.
acceptance_criteria:
- New keypair generated and held outside git
- Private key never committed in plaintext
- Public key extracted and available for the next step
- No reuse of existing cosign-signing-key material (fresh entropy)
notes: Manifest dir is op1st-pipelines-tokens, canonical ns is op1st-gitops, key is password-less. README documents the full rotation flow.
close_reason: Keypair generated on tmpfs, encrypted into manifests/applications/op1st-pipelines-tokens/cosign-signing-key-images.enc.yaml, public key published as .pub file. Commit 92c3a8f on branch cosign-image-keypair. Derived pub validated against stored pub (keypair mathematically consistent). No plaintext key material persisted.

title: Provision second cosign keypair to isolate release-tools image signing from forgejo-mcp artifact signing
description:
## What

Provision a second cosign keypair in the op1st-pipelines namespace so that:
- cosign-signing-key-images → used only to sign the release-tools container image
- cosign-signing-key-artifacts → used only to sign forgejo-mcp release artifact checksums

## Why

Currently both the release-tools image and forgejo-mcp release artifacts share the same cosign-signing-key Secret. The release-tools image is consumed at runtime by .tekton/tasks/cosign-sign-release.yaml, which also mounts the same key. A compromised release-tools image (via Containerfile change, transitive dep, or npm-package takeover) therefore has access to the artifact signing key and can forge signatures on attacker-supplied binaries.

Reference: forgejo-mcp-j52 (forgejo-mcp beads tracker, P1 security task).

## Required actions

1. Generate a new cosign keypair for image signing:
   cosign generate-key-pair
   Name the output keys: cosign-image.key / cosign-image.pub

2. Create or update the Secret in op1st-pipelines:
   - New secret: cosign-signing-key-images
   - Keys: cosign.key (the image-signing private key), cosign.password, cosign.pub
   - Existing secret cosign-signing-key remains for artifact signing (rename to cosign-signing-key-artifacts if clean split is desired)

3. Update the emberstack reflector ruleset to replicate cosign-signing-key-images into op1st-pipelines (same pattern as the existing cosign-signing-key reflector rule).

4. Update .tekton/release-tools/tasks/cosign-attach-sbom.yaml (or whichever task signs the release-tools image) to reference cosign-signing-key-images instead of cosign-signing-key.

5. Notify goern/forgejo-mcp so .tekton/tasks/cosign-sign-release.yaml secret ref can be updated to cosign-signing-key-artifacts if renamed.

## Acceptance criteria

- Two distinct Secrets exist in op1st-pipelines: one for image signing, one for artifact signing
- Reflector replicates both secrets
- release-tools image pipeline uses image key; forgejo-mcp release pipeline uses artifact key
- A compromised release-tools image cannot access the artifact signing key

title: Notify forgejo-mcp to repoint cosign-sign-release task at cosign-signing-key-artifacts
description:
Coordinate the downstream change in goern/forgejo-mcp so its release pipeline references the renamed artifact-only secret.

Steps:
- Open an issue / PR on goern/forgejo-mcp referencing forgejo-mcp-j52 and op1st-emea-b4mad-yn5
- Request update to .tekton/tasks/cosign-sign-release.yaml: change secretName from cosign-signing-key to cosign-signing-key-artifacts
- Provide cutover guidance: only merge after this repo's rename bead is applied and reflection has propagated
- After merge, verify a forgejo-mcp release pipeline run signs artifacts using the renamed Secret

Context: Parent op1st-emea-b4mad-yn5. Cross-repo coordination — gate on the rename bead landing first.
acceptance_criteria:
- Issue/PR opened on goern/forgejo-mcp with cross-links to forgejo-mcp-j52 and op1st-emea-b4mad-yn5
- forgejo-mcp .tekton/tasks/cosign-sign-release.yaml references cosign-signing-key-artifacts
- A forgejo-mcp release pipeline run completes and signatures verify with the artifact public key
- forgejo-mcp-j52 closed or progressed to its next phase

title: Rename cosign-signing-key to cosign-signing-key-artifacts
description:
Optional but recommended: rename the legacy shared key to make its remaining purpose (artifact signing only) explicit, now that the image-signing path uses a distinct secret.

Steps:
- Create new SealedSecret cosign-signing-key-artifacts.enc.yaml carrying the SAME key material as the current cosign-signing-key (no key rotation in this step — just a rename)
- Add reflector annotations matching the existing cosign-signing-key rule
- Apply; verify Secret cosign-signing-key-artifacts materializes and is reflected
- Coordinate cutover window with forgejo-mcp (see follow-up bead) before removing the old Secret
- After all consumers point at the new name, delete cosign-signing-key.enc.yaml and let ArgoCD prune

Context: Parent op1st-emea-b4mad-yn5. Cosmetic but reduces future confusion; do not perform until item 4 (release-tools cut-over) is verified, otherwise an in-flight pipeline could break.
acceptance_criteria:
- cosign-signing-key-artifacts SealedSecret exists and is reflected
- All consumers reference cosign-signing-key-artifacts
- Old cosign-signing-key SealedSecret removed from git
- ArgoCD prune completed; old Secret no longer present in any namespace

title: Stage op1st-release-token SealedSecret stub
description: Create encrypted placeholder file in op1st-pipelines-tokens; user fills real content; then seal + wire into kustomization.yaml + README.
close_reason: op1st-release-token sealed (Opaque, username+token, reflected to op1st-pipelines)

title: Replace cosign-signing-key with password-less keypair
description: Existing key carries password adjacent in same Secret — adds no security. Generate fresh keypair with empty COSIGN_PASSWORD, replace cosign.key/password/pub in SealedSecret. Pubkey changes; no verifiers depend on prior pubkey.
close_reason: cosign-signing-key replaced with fresh password-less keypair (COSIGN_PASSWORD=""). source-url annotation dropped, href repointed to op1st-gitops.

title: Add cosign-signing-key SealedSecret for op1st-pipelines
description: Mirror cosign signing key from goern/forgejo-mcp into op1st-pipelines-tokens dir. Encrypted at rest (SOPS), sealed for op1st-gitops, reflected to op1st-pipelines. Used by Tekton image signing.
close_reason: cosign-signing-key added: re-encrypted from forgejo-mcp commit 791ef6d, sealed for op1st-gitops, reflected to op1st-pipelines, provenance annotation set

title: Publish cosign public key file for verifiers
description: Extract cosign.pub from SealedSecret, write to repo so consumers can curl it. Pubkey is non-secret.
close_reason: cosign-signing-key.pub published in op1st-pipelines-tokens dir; verified matches cluster Secret

title: ArgoCD: helm valuesObject strips JSON null fields
description:
Argo Application spec.source.helm.valuesObject is typed (apiextensionsv1.JSON). At apiserver storage time, explicit JSON null values are normalized away. Overrides like 'key: null' silently vanish, leaving the Helm merge to fall back to chart defaults.

## Context
Hit during op1st-reflector chart override: tried to clear chart defaults runAsUser:1000 and fsGroup:2000 (OpenShift SCC rejected) by setting them null in valuesObject. last-applied-configuration annotation retained {runAsUser:null,fsGroup:null} as evidence intent reached apiserver. Live Application spec showed the keys gone. Helm merge restored chart defaults. ReplicaSet kept the same pod-template-hash across multiple 'fix' deploys - a tell-tale signal the override never reached the render. Fixed in commit 5dfbeab by switching to helm.values (YAML string).
acceptance_criteria: When overriding Helm chart values via Argo Application: prefer helm.values (YAML string) for any override that uses null/empty to delete a chart default. Workaround options: (a) helm.values string [cleanest]; (b) helm.parameters [string-typed, helm strict-types refuse for non-string scalars, mostly unusable here]; (c) post-render kustomize patch via Argo plugin pipeline [heavier]. Reserve valuesObject for cases where every override is a concrete value.

title: Harden op1st AppProject sourceRepos allowlist policy
description:
Current AppProject 'op1st' uses an exact-match allowlist for sourceRepos. Each new chart/git source requires a manifest edit (e.g., emberstack helm-charts added in commit db06e19). Evaluate two alternatives for tighter posture vs ergonomics.

## Context
Options to evaluate: (1) sourceRepos: ['*'] combined with sourceNamespaces restriction - permissive on repo URL but locks down which namespaces can create Applications referencing this project; (2) keep strict allowlist but add a default-deny base project plus explicit per-source allowlists per Application area. Current allowlist pattern is correct but needs maintenance discipline. Decision needed: stay with allowlist (status quo) or switch to one of the alternatives.
acceptance_criteria: Written decision recorded in repo (ADR or this bead). If staying with allowlist: document the maintenance step in a CONTRIBUTING/runbook. If switching: PR with updated AppProject + tested sync of an in-scope and out-of-scope Application.

title: ArgoCD: two AppProjects share name across control planes
description:
OpenShift cluster runs two ArgoCD instances: cluster-mgmt openshift-gitops and workload op1st-gitops. Each has its own AppProject CRD (namespace-scoped). AppProject lookup: (Application.metadata.namespace -> matching ArgoCD instance -> AppProject in that ns). Easy to edit the wrong one if you only grep by name.

## Context
Both projects literally named 'op1st'. File paths: manifests/applications/openshift-gitops/projects/op1st.yaml (cluster-mgmt) vs manifests/applications/op1st-gitops/projects/op1st.yaml (workload). Hit during reflector chart sourceRepos fix (commit db06e19) - reflector App lives in openshift-gitops ns so the openshift-gitops/projects/op1st.yaml was the one to edit.
acceptance_criteria: When changing AppProject settings: identify which Argo instance owns the Application by its metadata.namespace, then edit the AppProject in the matching control-plane projects/ dir. Never assume name uniqueness.
